world.title: Travellers Lead African Safari Bookings in 2026: US Dominance Revealed
world.description: American travellers lead African safari bookings in 2026, commanding nearly half of all reservations. New data reveals a surge in immersive wilderness experiences and extended remote camp stays across the continent.

world.title: US says it could back Argentina’s claim to the Falklands as ‘punishment’ for UK
world.description: Argentina has called for renewed talks over the future of the Falkland Islands after the US suggested it could support Buenos Aires’s claims of sovereignty.

world.title: Twelve injured as Chinese driver’s BMW wrecks Sukhumvit KFC
world.description: Twelve people were injured after a BMW driven by a Chinese man crashed into a KFC restaurant inside a Bangchak petrol station on Sukhumvit 62.

world.title: Gunshots at Trump attended event shake US again
world.description: All eyes had been on the annual White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington last night for different reasons, but this changed when the US president was evacuated following gunshots.

world.title: Trump cancels Pakistan talks trip, says Iran war on hold
world.description: Trump said he scrapped the visit after being unimpressed with Tehran's negotiating position, adding that a revised proposal followed within minutes of his decision.

world.title: Somalia signs satellite cooperation deal with China at NewSpace Africa Conference
world.description: Mogadishu (HOL) — Somalia has signed a cooperation agreement with China to expand the use of satellite technology in national development, marking a step toward strengthening its role in emerging space and data systems.

world.title: Twin Brothers Die in Each Other’s Arms After Tragic Power Incident Days to Their Birthday
world.description: Twin brothers, 22, were found dead in each other’s arms after a tragic electricity accident while training birds, in a suspected attempt to save one another.

world.title: How Iran is wielding the Strait of Hormuz as leverage
world.description: US signals openness to talks, but Iran is buying time, banking on global reliance on Persian Gulf energy and leverage over the Strait of Hormuz.

business.title: Giant €35m moving statue on docks could be new landmark attraction for Dublin
business.description: Dublin could be in line for a new landmark attraction — the world’s largest moving human-shaped statue.

business.title: Triona McCarthy: These new spring launches will help you tackle everything from ‘tech neck’ to dull skin
business.description: As a beauty expert, I’m always wiping and swiping, testing and then sometimes investing myself in the products that earn a permanent place on my bathroom shelf. These are the ones I’m reaching for right now and of course it’s my beauty duty to spill the Tri!

business.title: ‘Material uncertainty’ exists at Spotlight Oral Care, despite its return to profit, say auditors
business.description: The auditor for Spotlight Oral Care has indicated a “material uncertainty” regarding the dental product company’s future, relating to shares held by a key investor.

business.title: Stoke-on-Trent attraction now shut on Sundays 'for the foreseeable'
business.description: There are 'very low visitor numbers' on Sundays

business.description: The OpenAI CEO, 41, tried his hand at Gen Alpha internet slang and it didn't really go well.

business.title: Sam Altman, your millennial is showing
business.description: Even as a perma-online millennial, I can relate to the struggle OpenAI CEO Sam Altman faced on Saturday, when he tried and failed to use a Gen Alpha internet slang term correctly. | Business Insider Africa

business.title: Trump hosts $TRUMP token buyers at Mar-a-Lago
business.description: Donald Trump hosted top $TRUMP token holders at Mar-a-Lago as scrutiny of the Trump family’s wider crypto ventures intensified.

business.title: Khanh Hoa Tourism: A Strategic Leap in International Arrivals for Early 2026
business.description: Boasting a significant surge in foreign visitors through its international aviation gateway, Khanh Hoa province reaffirms its status as a top-tier destination, skillfully navigating global volatility to reach its ambitious annual targets.

science.title: Webb Finds Water-Ice Clouds on Nearby Super-Jupiter
science.description: The giant planets in our solar system—Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune—have challenged our understanding of planetary formation and evolution. Specifically, their atmospheric formations and compositions have provided awe-inspiring images from spacecraft and given scientists key insights into the interior mechanisms of these massive worlds. But what about exoplanets? What can their atmospheres teach scientists about their formation, evolution, composition, and interior mechanisms? And how do longstanding exoplanet models stack up against the real thing?

science.title: The world's darkest skies are under pressure in the Atacama Desert
science.description: The Atacama Desert in Chile offers some of the clearest views of the universe, thanks to its dry climate, high altitude and isolation from light pollution.

science.title: NASA's SpaceX Crew-13 pays homage to Apollo 13 on mission patch
science.description: NASA has assigned its first crew to launch on a mission "13" since Apollo 13 "had a problem" on the way to the moon. Rather than give into triskaidekaphobia, the crew is embracing it by including nods to the Apollo 13 patch within their own Crew-13 insignia.

science.title: Warming waters are supercharging an invasive salmon predator in Alaska
science.description: As Alaska’s rivers warm, invasive northern pike are becoming noticeably more voracious. Scientists discovered that pike of all ages are eating more fish, with young pike increasing consumption by over 60%. Warmer water speeds up their metabolism, pushing them to hunt more. This growing appetite could spell trouble for struggling salmon populations.

science.title: Aggressive “hulk” lizards are wiping out millions of years of evolution
science.description: For ages, wall lizards coexisted in three distinct color types, each with its own strategy for survival. Now, a powerful green variant is taking over. These dominant “Hulk” lizards are outcompeting the others, causing yellow and orange morphs to vanish. It’s a dramatic reminder that evolution can flip the script much faster than expected.

science.title: Physicists Suggest Mysterious "Fifth Force" May Exist In Our Solar System
science.description: The study says that large cosmological surveys like Euclid and DESI can provide hints of new physics by looking at billions of galaxies.

science.title: Wildbuzz | Lamps lit by the night
science.description: About 2,200 species of fireflies have been scientifically assessed across the Earth; these drifting embers across time and space have been described by Cate Hibbitt as an “accessible biological magic: jewels of light glinting in summer evenings throughout the world”

science.title: Proposed "sky mirrors" would brighten nights by reflecting sunshine
science.description: California company plans to place 4,000 "sky mirrors" in Earth orbit that will brighten skies over cities, alarming astronomers.

This has caused foreign investors to sell Indian stocks and invest in countries with a strong tech sector, as the weight of tech […]

ai.title: Elon Musk Drops Major Allegations Against OpenAI, Sam Altman Before Trial
ai.description: Elon Musk has significantly reduced the scope of his lawsuit against OpenAI, dropping numerous fraud allegations just before the trial's start. This decision allows only two claims-unjust enrichment and breach of charitable trust, to proceed. , Technology & Science, Times Now

ai.title: Despite recovery, why Indian stocks are trailing global indices
ai.description: The AI investment boom that carried several countries’ stock markets on a historic ride before the West Asia war has returned. And Indian markets have, again, been left standing at the gate.

ai.title: Context decay, orchestration drift, and the rise of silent failures in AI systems
ai.description: Enterprises are obsessing over model accuracy while ignoring the infrastructure layer where AI systems actually break.
